content.title: Time Management Strategy
content.body:
**Exam timing breakdown:**
- Total exam time: approximately 40 minutes
- Listening: ~15 minutes (fixed, controlled by audio)
- Reading: ~17 minutes (flexible, self-paced)
- Answer sheet transfer: ~5-8 minutes

**Strategic time management:**

For listening: You cannot control pace, so focus on stayin

content.title: Reading Part 2 Format
content.body:
**Reading Part 2** (Questions 26-30) presents **sentence matching** tasks.

You see two columns: one with sentence beginnings or questions, and another with sentence endings or responses. You must match items from the two columns to create logical, grammatically correct pairings.

This tests underst

content.title: Reading Part 1 Format
content.body:
**Reading Part 1** (Questions 21-25) tests your ability to match **pictures with words or short phrases**.

You see several pictures and several Chinese words or short phrases. You must match each picture with the corresponding written Chinese.

This tests basic reading vocabulary and character reco

content.title: Listening Part 3 Format
content.body:
**Listening Part 3** (Questions 11-15) presents **short dialogues** between two people.

For each question, you hear a **brief conversation** (played twice) and then a **question about the conversation**. You must choose the correct answer from several pictures.

This tests your ability to understan

content.title: Exam Day Task Recognition
content.body:
**Scenario:** You're taking the HSK 1 exam. For each description below, identify which part of the exam you're working on.

Being able to quickly recognize which task type you're facing helps you apply the right strategy immediately without wasting time or becoming confused.

content.title: Vocabulary and Task Type Relationships
content.body:
The HSK 1 vocabulary of 150 words appears throughout different task types, but certain word categories are more common in specific parts:

**Concrete nouns** (人, 书, 水, etc.): Common in picture-matching tasks (Listening Part 1, Reading Part 1)

**Verbs and adjectives** (看, 大, 好, etc.): Frequently app

content.title: Listening Part 4 Format
content.body:
**Listening Part 4** (Questions 16-20) tests comprehension of **questions and appropriate responses**.

For each item, you hear a **question or statement** (played twice). You must choose which of several options (usually given as pictures or short written phrases) is the most appropriate response o

content.title: Task Type Progression
content.body:
The four parts in each section follow a progression from simpler to more complex tasks:

**Listening progression:**
1. Single word → picture (isolated vocabulary)
2. Sentence → picture judgment (sentence comprehension)
3. Dialogue → information question (conversational context)
4. Question → appropr

content.title: Exam Section Comparison
content.body:
Understanding the differences between the listening and reading sections helps you prepare effectively:

**Listening section characteristics:**
- Audio played at fixed pace (no control over speed)
- Each item played twice
- Tests spoken vocabulary and natural speech comprehension
- Requires immediat

content.title: Listening Section Structure
content.body:
The listening section (听力) has 20 questions divided into **four parts**:
- Part 1: Questions 1-5
- Part 2: Questions 6-10
- Part 3: Questions 11-15
- Part 4: Questions 16-20

Each part tests a different skill using different task types. All listening materials are played twice, giving you two chance

content.title: HSK 1 Exam Overview
content.body:
The HSK (Hanyu Shuiping Kaoshi, 汉语水平考试) is China's standardized test for Mandarin proficiency. HSK 1 is the beginner level, testing basic vocabulary and grammar.

The exam contains **40 questions total** divided into two sections:
- **听力 (tīnglì)** — Listening: 20 questions
- **阅读 (yuèdú)** — Readin

content.title: Listening Part 2 Format
content.body:
**Listening Part 2** (Questions 6-10) tests comprehension of short sentences through **true/false judgments**.

For each question, you see a **picture** and hear a **sentence** (played twice). You must decide if the sentence correctly describes the picture or not. You mark:
- ✓ (true/correct) if the

content.title: Scoring Strategy and Pass Requirements
content.body:
With a passing score of 120/200, you need to answer **24 out of 40 questions correctly** (60% accuracy). This means you can miss up to 16 questions and still pass.

**Strategic implications:**
- There is no penalty for guessing—always answer every question
- You don't need perfection; consistent per

content.title: Listening Part 1 Format
content.body:
**Listening Part 1** (Questions 1-5) tests your ability to match spoken words to pictures. 

For each question, you hear a **single word or short phrase** (played twice). You see **several pictures** and must choose which picture matches what you heard.

This tests basic vocabulary recognition in sp

content.title: Reading Part 4 Format
content.body:
**Reading Part 4** (Questions 36-40) presents a **sentence with a picture** or **short passage**.

For each question, you read a sentence and must choose which picture matches its meaning, or answer a simple comprehension question about what you read.

This tests reading comprehension of complete se

content.title: Reading Section Structure
content.body:
The reading section (阅读) has 20 questions divided into **four parts**:
- Part 1: Questions 21-25
- Part 2: Questions 26-30
- Part 3: Questions 31-35
- Part 4: Questions 36-40

Unlike the listening section where audio is played, you work through reading questions at your own pace. Each part uses diff

content.title: HSK 1 Scoring System
content.body:
Each question on the HSK 1 exam is worth **5 points**, giving a maximum of **200 points total**:
- Listening section: 100 points maximum (20 questions × 5 points)
- Reading section: 100 points maximum (20 questions × 5 points)

The passing score is **120 points out of 200**. This means you need to a

- Part 4 (Questions 16-20): 4 correct

content.title: Reading Part 3 Format
content.body:
**Reading Part 3** (Questions 31-35) provides **short dialogues or sentences with a blank**.

For each item, you see a conversation or sentence with one word or phrase missing. You must choose the correct option to fill in the blank from multiple choices.

This tests your understanding of context, v
